reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully captures the informal statement. The sample space `SM n` correctly encodes matrices whose columns are permutations of {1,2,3} (since the set of column entries equals {1,2,3}, forcing a 3-element column to be a permutation). `Srowsums` is the set of row sums. Event 1 (`sumsprog`) correctly captures "b = a+1 and c = a+2" after sorting, since this forces the sorted sums to be three consecutive integers, equivalent to the set being {a, a+1, a+2}. Event 2 (`encard = 1`) correctly captures "a = b = c". Because the probabilistic description in the informal statement ("all orders equally likely, independent columns") induces a uniform distribution on SM n, the probability ratio P(E1) ≥ 4·P(E2) is equivalent to the cardinality ratio |E1| ≥ 4·|E2|, which is what the Lean asserts. The existential n ≥ 1995 also matches.
